publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-03-23 23:53:51 -0400.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, Maps/Charts, Newspapers, Printed Materials, Publications
did.abstract: Correspondence, reports, blueprints, whiteprints, maps, pamphlets, monographs, and newspaper clippings mainly concerning his association (1922-1937) with John Nolen.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Justin R. Hartzog graduated from Cornell University in 1917, and in 1922 received a Masters Degree in Landscape Design from Cornell.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, reports, blueprints, whiteprints, maps, pamphlets, monographs, and newspaper clippings mainly concerning his association (1922-1937) with John Nolen, the city planner of Cambridge, Massachusetts, and his own consulting practice in Cambridge and Fayetteville, Ohio, on projects at Dubuque, Iowa; Little Rock, Arkansas; Roanoke, Virginia; Lancaster, Pennsylvania; Columbus, Georgia; Elizabethton, Tennessee; San Antonio, Texas; Milton, Massachusetts; and elsewhere; his chairmanship of the Massachusetts Federation of Planning Boards and the Cambridge Planning Board; his state planning activities as consultant from the National Resources Planning Board for New Hampshire and Rhode Island, for the Maine State Planning Board, and the New England Regional Planning Commission; and his consulting work for agencies of the federal government, including the U.S. Housing Authority (1935-42), the Division of Defense Housing Coordination for the New England Region (1940-41), the Division of Urban Studies of the National Housing Agency (1942-44), and the Resettlement Administration for Greenhills, Ohio, where he was chief town planner (1935-37) and consultant after its sale by the government (1947-63).

Also included are letters, reports, notes, and other papers concerning his studies at Cornell University; his active and reserve service with the U.S. Army (1917-41); lectures he gave at Cornell University, Massachusetts Institute of Technology, Harvard University, and Ohio State University; and his activities in professional organizations, in particular the American Society of Civil Engineers and the American Society of Landscape Architects. Also, briefs and depositions (1960-66) in the Greenhills Home Owners Corporation suit. The major correspondents are Jacob Crane Sr., John Nolen, Sr., and Bradford Williams; there are also occasional letters from Frederick Adams, Tracy B. Augur, Harland Bartholomew, Frederick Bigger, Arthur Comey, Earle S. Draper, Charles Eliot, Carl Feiss, Harold Lewis, Benton MacKaye, Howard K. Menhinick, Paul Oppermann, Ladislas Segoe, Clarence S. Stein, and Raymond Unwin.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Justin R. Hartzog papers, #2407. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: City planners.
Regional planning.
Housing.
City planning.
controlaccess.corpname: United States. Division of Defense Housing Coordination for the New England Region
United States. National Housing Agency. Division of Urban Studies
United States. National Resources Planning Board

unittitle.extref: Link to a copy of the printed finding aid. Searchable PDF.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Not all the material in the collection is covered by this guide.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph, greeting cards, announcements, invitations, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Relating to Hartzog, his father and miscellaneous

did.unittitle: Cambridge School of Architecture and Landscape Architecture
Massachusetts Institute of Technology , visiting critic and Associate Professor
Charlotte North Carolina - Jones, Edwin L.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Glenn L. Martin Company - Robert L. Henry

did.unittitle: National Bureau of Casualty and Surety Underwriters
National Commission on the Housing Emergency Incorporated
New York, Plan for its Environs

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: 1922-1926: Technical Assistant in town planning and subdivision. 1926-1937: Associate in practice of town and regional planning

did.unittitle: Reading (Wyomissing Park) - includes 3 boxes of aerial surveys)
Report on Major Streets and Highways
Dubuque Planning and Zoning Commission Minutes
Preliminary report - Revision of the 1934 Ordinance
Dock Commission - Industrial Park, Peosta Highway
Report on expansion of the city area
Chamber of Commerce Urban Development Commission
Traffic Forecast - Iowa State Highway Commission
Revision of Zoning Regulations - Residential Districts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscripts regarding zoning and planning, newspaper clippings, pamphlets and aerial surveys

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: 1928-1930: Prepared City Plan. 1931: Prepared plan for Allsopp Park in Little Rock. 1933: Zoning Act

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: 1927: Reconnaissance Survey and Planning Report. 1928: Zoning Ordinances, street plans, city planning report. 1931: Long Term Financial Program for Public Improvements and Report.

did.unittitle: Report on Long Term Budget Program

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American Glanzhoff Corporation - Happy Valley development

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Hertzog applied for a job as town planner, was offered it, and turned it down.

did.unittitle: Lecture by Earle S. Draper, Director, Division of Planning and Housing

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: 1935: Designed a pleasure and health resort for the Hoblitzelle Holding Corporation.

did.unittitle: Report on Increase of Minimum Lot Area and Frontage - C District
Report on Potentialities of Development - Houghton's Pond Area
Report on Long Term Residential Zoning Needs in Milton
General and detail layout plans for proposed housing projects

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Legal matters, meetings, program, and annual reports (drafts)

did.unittitle: National Resources Planning Board (and its predecessors)

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: 1933-1943: Consultant to New Hampshire. 1943-1948: Consultant to Rhode Island.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material specific to Hertzog in New Hampshire and Rhode Island is in appropriate boxes.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: 1933-1943: State Planning Consultant, National Resources Planning Board

did.unittitle: Establishing of State Planning Board funds
Survey of City Planning and Zoning
Possibility of Directing Settlement of Land under Private Ownership
Federal Emergency Relief Administration - National Emergency Council

Suggestions for Specific Program of Planning Activity
Suggestions for Securing Extension of Planning Assistance
Extension of Public Ownership of Land

Act to create New England Regional Planning Commission
Act to create Maine State Planning Board
Draft to create Vermont State Planning Commission

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: 1943-1948: State Planning Board Consultant, National Resources Planning Board

Roadside Control and Improvement Advisory Committee

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: 1935-1940: Chief Town Planner. 1947- Town Planning Consultant.

did.unittitle: Specifications for Construction of a Community Building
Report and Program for Extension in Development

Files made available to attorneys in suit of December 1961

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: 1940-1942: Regional Coordinator Special Consultant for Region I - New England

did.unittitle: Manual for Planning of Defense Housing - drafts
Division of Defense Housing Coordination (Office for Emergency Management)

Other Agencies (includes Office of Defense Transportation)
Las Vegas (see also Division of Defense Housing)
Ohio Supreme Court: Greenhills Home Owners Corporation, plaintiff vs. Village of Greenhills, Ohio, defendant

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Appeal from Court of Appeals. Record Volumes I, II, III

did.unittitle: Ohio Supreme Court: Brief of Defendant-Appellants
United States Supreme Court: Greenhills Home Owners Corporation, petitioner vs. Village of Greenhills, Ohio, respondents
Ohio Court of Common Pleas: Greenhills Home Owners Corporation, relator vs. Village of Greenhills, Ohio, respondents. Deposition of John Taylor Egan.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Plans and drawings have not yet been processed. See PDF guide at the top of this guide for information about plans housed in the Rare and Manuscript Collection.
